req.title: Hr-Admin creates policy-document
req.statement: An authenticated hr-admin shall be able to create a policy-document through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers: (1) successful creation by an authenticated hr-admin with all mandatory fields (EP/STC positive path), (2) form-level validation rejection when a mandatory field is omitted (NEG/DT error path), (3) boundary on the title field at its maximum allowed length (BVA), (4) 401 when unauthenticated (NEG), and (5) 403 when a non-admin role submits (NEG/DT). Five tests cover all cited heuristics and satisfy the minimum-three requirement with additional depth. Each test uses a <hr_admin_session> or <anon_session> fixture, a <create_policy_document_via_ui> helper, and an <audit_record_exists> helper to keep executables under 40 lines.

tests.name: test_hr_admin_creates_policy_document_all_fields
preconditions: No existing document with the same title in the test dataset
actions: Fill all mandatory fields with valid representative values
expected: Response body contains a non-empty entity id
An audit record is written for the creation event
The new document appears in the policy-documents list
trace: R-WEB-053: positive path – authenticated hr-admin creates document
R-WEB-053: audit record must be written on success
R-WEB-053: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_hr_admin_creates_policy_document_all_fields(hr_admin_session, api_client):
    payload = {
        "title": "Annual Leave Policy",
        "category": "HR",
        "effective_date": "2024-01-01",
        "content": "Policy content text."
    }
    response = <create_policy_document_via_ui>(hr_admin_session, payload)
    assert response.status_code in (200, 201)
    entity_id = response.json().get("id")
    assert entity_id, "Expected non-empty entity id in response"
    assert <audit_record_exists>(api_client, entity_id, action="CREATE")
    list_resp = api_client.get("/policy-documents")
    ids = [doc["id"] for doc in list_resp.json().get("items", [])]
    assert entity_id in ids

tests.name: test_create_policy_document_missing_mandatory_title_returns_400
actions: Fill all mandatory fields EXCEPT title
expected: Response body contains a validation error referencing the title field
trace: R-WEB-053: mandatory fields must be validated
R-WEB-053: error pathway 400 on validation error
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_create_policy_document_missing_mandatory_title_returns_400(hr_admin_session, api_client):
    payload = {
        "title": "",
        "category": "HR",
        "effective_date": "2024-01-01",
        "content": "Policy content text."
    }
    response = <create_policy_document_via_ui>(hr_admin_session, payload)
    assert response.status_code == 400
    error_body = response.json()
    field_errors = error_body.get("errors", {})
    assert "title" in field_errors, "Expected validation error for 'title' field"
    assert not <audit_record_exists>(api_client, entity_id=None, action="CREATE", recent_seconds=5)

tests.name: test_create_policy_document_title_at_max_boundary_length
preconditions: Max allowed title length is 255 characters (from field spec)
actions: Fill remaining mandatory fields with valid values
expected: Response body contains a non-empty entity id
Persisted document title equals the 255-character string
trace: R-WEB-053: mandatory field title boundary at maximum length
R-WEB-053: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest

MAX_TITLE_LEN = 255

def test_create_policy_document_title_at_max_boundary_length(hr_admin_session, api_client):
    max_title = "A" * MAX_TITLE_LEN
    payload = {
        "title": max_title,
        "category": "HR",
        "effective_date": "2024-06-01",
        "content": "Boundary test content."
    }
    response = <create_policy_document_via_ui>(hr_admin_session, payload)
    assert response.status_code in (200, 201)
    entity_id = response.json().get("id")
    assert entity_id
    detail_resp = api_client.get(f"/policy-documents/{entity_id}")
    assert detail_resp.json()["title"] == max_title
    assert <audit_record_exists>(api_client, entity_id, action="CREATE")

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_create_policy_document
actions: Attempt to POST to /policy-documents without authentication credentials
expected: Response body indicates authentication is required
trace: Decision table row: unauthenticated × any role → 401
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_create_policy_document(anon_session, api_client):
    payload = {
        "title": "Unauthorized Policy",
        "category": "HR",
        "effective_date": "2024-01-01",
        "content": "Should not be created."
    }
    response = <create_policy_document_via_ui>(anon_session, payload)
    assert response.status_code == 401
    assert not <audit_record_exists>(api_client, entity_id=None, action="CREATE", recent_seconds=5)

tests.name: test_non_admin_role_forbidden_from_creating_policy_document
preconditions: User is authenticated with role: employee (not hr-admin)
actions: Navigate to /policy-documents as an employee-role user
Attempt to submit the creation form with valid field values
trace: R-WEB-053: error pathway 403 if role forbidden
State transition: authenticated(employee) → attempt create → forbidden state
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_non_admin_role_forbidden_from_creating_policy_document(employee_session, api_client):
    payload = {
        "title": "Forbidden Policy Attempt",
        "category": "HR",
        "effective_date": "2024-01-01",
        "content": "Should be rejected."
    }
    response = <create_policy_document_via_ui>(employee_session, payload)
    assert response.status_code == 403
    error_body = response.json()
    assert "forbidden" in error_body.get("message", "").lower() or "permission" in error_body.get("message", "").lower()
    assert not <audit_record_exists>(api_client, entity_id=None, action="CREATE", recent_seconds=5)
